conversation.message: Good morning. It's good to see you again. How have things been going for you lately?
It's been tough. Just a lot on my plate. Work has been stressful, and I’m trying to figure out how to make ends meet. I feel like I'm just holding on by a thread most days.

I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like you're really struggling to keep up with everything. Can you tell me a bit more about what's been going on at work that's causing you so much stress? Is it a particular project or deadline that's got you feeling overwhelmed?

It's just... everything. The boss is breathing down my neck, and I'm not meeting my numbers. I feel like I'm not doing my job right. I used to be on top of things, but now I just feel like I'm drowning.

It sounds like you're feeling a lot of pressure and responsibility, and it's taking a toll on your sense of accomplishment and confidence. That can be really tough to deal with. Have you had to make any changes to your daily routine or habits lately? Are you finding time for things that bring you joy or relaxation?
No... I haven't had time for anything else. I used to read or watch TV after work, but now I just collapse on the couch. I don't have the energy to do anything else.

It sounds like you're really feeling drained and exhausted. That can be a really tough place to be. It's like you're running on empty and don't have the resources to do the things that bring you joy or help you unwind. Has it been hard to find time for yourself or do things that you used to enjoy?
Yeah... it’s hard to find the energy to even think about it. It feels like a chore to do the things I used to like. I just feel... done.

It sounds like you're feeling really burnt out and disconnected from the things that used to bring you pleasure. That can be a really tough feeling to deal with. It's like you're just going through the motions of daily life without any sense of purpose or joy. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "done"? What does that feeling like for you?
It's just... a grey haze. Everything feels pointless. I used to have goals, but now I just feel like I'm just trying to survive until the end of the day. It’s a really sad feeling.

It sounds like you're feeling a sense of hopelessness and despair. It's like you're just trying to get through each day without any sense of direction or purpose. That can be a really tough and isolating feeling. It's like you're just going through the motions without any sense of joy or fulfillment.
Yeah... it feels like I'm just a ghost, just existing. I don't know how to get out of this rut.

It sounds like you're feeling really disconnected and lost. It's like you're just going through the motions of daily life without any sense of purpose or direction. That can be a really tough and scary place to be. It's like you're just trying to find your way out of this darkness, but you're not sure where to turn or how to get there.
Yeah... it feels like I'm just floating in the middle of the ocean with no life jacket.

That's a really powerful and evocative image. It sounds like you're feeling completely adrift and without any sense of control or direction. It's like you're just trying to stay afloat and not sink beneath the surface. Thank you for sharing this with me. I think I have a good understanding now. We can discuss this further at our next appointment.
Good morning. It's good to see you again. How have things been going for you lately?
It’s been tough. I just lost my job last week, and my mom passed away a few months ago. It feels like everything is falling apart.

I'm so sorry to hear that. Losing a job and a loved one in a short period of time can be incredibly overwhelming. It sounds like you're feeling a lot of weight on your shoulders right now. Can you tell me a bit more about what's been going on since you lost your job? How have you been taking care of yourself during this time?
I haven't been sleeping much. I just lie awake and think about everything I should be doing differently. I don't know how I’m supposed to find a new job or just... I don't know, adult, I guess.

It sounds like you're feeling a lot of anxiety and uncertainty about your future. Not sleeping can be really tough, and it's like your mind is racing with all these thoughts and worries. Have you been doing anything that brings you some comfort or relaxation during the day? Like, are you taking a walk, reading a book, or spending time with friends or family?
I try, but I don't really have the energy. I just stay on my phone or watch TV. It feels easier than thinking about anything else, I guess.

It can be really hard to muster up the energy to do things when you're feeling down. And sometimes, just zoning out on the phone or TV can feel like a temporary escape from all the worries and emotions swirling around. Have you been able to connect with anyone about how you're feeling, or is it more of a solo experience for you right now?
Just my sister, really. She lives a few hours away, so we mostly talk on the phone. She gets it, but it’s not the same as having someone close by who understands.

It's great that you have your sister to talk to, even if it's not in person. Sometimes just having someone to share your thoughts and feelings with can be a big help. But I can imagine how it would be nice to have someone close by who understands what you're going through. Do you have any hobbies or activities that you used to enjoy before all this happened, that you're not doing as much anymore?
I used to like hiking, but I haven't gone in weeks. I just don't have the energy for it lately.

It sounds like hiking was something that brought you joy and helped you clear your mind. Not having the energy to do it can be really tough. Has it been hard for you to find the motivation to do anything that used to bring you pleasure, or is it more that you just feel physically drained?
I guess it's both. I miss doing things I used to love, but I don't have the energy to do them. It's like I'm just going through the motions without actually living.

It sounds like you're feeling a sense of disconnection from your usual self and the things that brought you joy. It's like you're just existing, rather than truly living. That can be a really tough and isolating feeling. Thank you for sharing this with me. I think I have a good understanding now. We can discuss this further at our next appointment.
